action.reasoning: Observation: We have ~1.13h (68 min) and 12 iterations left. Best mIoU is 0.5132 (iter 16, multi-scale TTA). Iteration 18's batched predict.py crashed with SIGKILL because it accumulated full [150,H,W] logits for all 500 images per GPU in RAM. Analysis: To beat/match 0.5132 within the time budget, I must run a safe multi-scale TTA inference that finishes well under 68 min. The fix is to process images sequentially (one at a time) per GPU worker, accumulating logits for only a single image in CPU RAM before saving its PNG. To recover speed, I batch original+flip together in one forward pass (7 forwards per image instead of 14). Memory is safe because only one image accumulator lives on CPU at a time. 4 GPUs × 500 images × 7 forwards ≈ 3,500 forwards/GPU, which at ~0.15 sec each is ~9 min GPU time + overhead, so total wall-clock ~25–35 min. Decision: Rewrite predict.py with this robust single-image-accumulation, batched-flip approach, using the same 7 scales and max_size=3200 that yielded the best score. Do NOT touch model.py or train.py. Launch inference immediately.
action.command: python -c "from model import SegmentationModel; import torch; m=SegmentationModel(150); print('Model OK, params', sum(p.numel() for p in m.parameters()))" && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output predictions.json
